pattern: A level-order traversal that keeps the running maximum per level produces one largest value for each depth in top-to-bottom order.
hints: Each answer entry corresponds to one level of the tree, in order from the root down.
Traverse the tree level by level, looking at every node on a level together.
For each level take the maximum of that level's values and append it to the result.

edge_inputs.description: empty tree gives an empty list
balanced tree with holes on a level
right child larger than the left forces a max comparison
negative values pick the least-negative per row
left-skewed chain yields one value per level

stress.description: random level-order tree with holes and wide value range
stress.note: n may be zero to cover the empty-tree path
